questions.stem: Under ASC 230, interest paid by a business entity is classified in which section of the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Operating activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: Investing activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: Financing activities

choices.label: D
choices.text: Either operating or financing at the entity's election

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-17, interest paid is classified as an operating activity under U.S. GAAP. This differs from IFRS, which allows classification as either operating or financing. Interest received is also classified as operating under U.S. GAAP.
questions.explanationJa: 米国GAAPでは、支払利息は営業活動に分類される。IFRSでは営業活動または財務活動のいずれかを選択できるが、米国GAAPでは選択の余地はない。受取利息も同様に営業活動である。

questions.stem: When preparing the operating section using the indirect method, which of the following requires an addition to net income?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Increase in accounts receivable

choices.label: B
choices.text: Decrease in accounts payable

choices.label: C
choices.text: Depreciation expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: Gain on sale of equipment

questions.explanation: Depreciation expense is a non-cash charge that reduces net income but does not affect cash. Under the indirect method, it is added back to net income to arrive at cash from operations. An increase in receivables is subtracted, a decrease in payables is subtracted, and gains on sale of equipment are subtracted (the cash proceeds are classified as investing).
questions.explanationJa: 減価償却費は現金支出を伴わない非現金費用である。間接法では純利益に加算して営業活動CFを算出する。売掛金増加は減算、買掛金減少は減算、設備売却益は減算（現金収入全額が投資活動に計上されるため）。

questions.stem: Which of the following transactions should be disclosed as a noncash investing and financing activity rather than reported in the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Payment of a cash dividend

choices.label: B
choices.text: Acquisition of equipment by issuing a note payable

choices.label: C
choices.text: Proceeds from issuing common stock

choices.label: D
choices.text: Purchase of treasury stock for cash

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-50-3, noncash investing and financing activities are excluded from the statement of cash flows but must be disclosed separately. Acquiring equipment by issuing a note payable involves no cash exchange and is therefore a noncash transaction requiring disclosure.
questions.explanationJa: 非現金の投資・財務活動はCF計算書から除外し、別途開示する。手形発行による設備取得は現金の授受がないため非現金取引に該当し、注記での開示が求められる。

questions.stem: Company K reported net income of $200,000. During the year, accounts receivable increased by $15,000, inventory decreased by $8,000, accounts payable decreased by $12,000, and depreciation was $25,000. What is cash provided by operating activities using the indirect method?
questions.explanation: Starting with net income of $200,000: add back depreciation $25,000; subtract increase in AR ($15,000); add decrease in inventory $8,000; subtract decrease in AP ($12,000). Total: $200,000 + $25,000 - $15,000 + $8,000 - $12,000 = $206,000.

questions.stem: Entity L sold equipment with a book value of $80,000 for $95,000 cash. How is this transaction reported on the statement of cash flows using the indirect method?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $95,000 inflow in investing; $15,000 gain subtracted in operating

choices.label: B
choices.text: $95,000 inflow in investing only

choices.label: C
choices.text: $80,000 inflow in investing; $15,000 gain in operating

choices.label: D
choices.text: $15,000 inflow in investing; $80,000 inflow in operating

questions.explanation: The entire $95,000 cash proceeds are reported as an investing inflow. Under the indirect method, the $15,000 gain (which is already included in net income) must be subtracted from net income in the operating section to avoid double-counting, since the full cash effect is shown in investing.
questions.explanationJa: 売却代金$95,000の全額を投資活動の流入として報告する。間接法では、$15,000の売却益は既に純利益に含まれているため、二重計上を避けるために営業活動で純利益から減算する。現金効果の全額は投資活動に表示される。

questions.stem: Which of the following is classified as a financing activity on the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Cash received from customers

choices.label: B
choices.text: Cash paid to acquire another company

choices.label: C
choices.text: Cash received from issuing bonds

choices.label: D
choices.text: Cash paid for interest on bonds

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-14 and 45-15, financing activities include proceeds from issuing equity instruments, proceeds from issuing debt, and repayments of debt. Cash received from issuing bonds is a financing inflow. Cash paid for interest is operating; cash paid to acquire a company is investing.
questions.explanationJa: 財務活動には、持分証券の発行収入、負債の発行収入、負債の返済が含まれる。社債発行による現金受取は財務活動の流入である。利息支払は営業活動、企業買収のための支払は投資活動に分類される。

questions.stem: Under the direct method of reporting cash flows from operating activities, cash received from customers is computed as:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Sales revenue + decrease in accounts receivable (or - increase)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Sales revenue + increase in accounts receivable

choices.label: C
choices.text: Net income + depreciation

choices.label: D
choices.text: Sales revenue - cost of goods sold

questions.explanation: Under the direct method, cash collected from customers equals sales revenue adjusted for the change in accounts receivable. If AR decreased, more cash was collected than revenue recognized (add the decrease). If AR increased, less cash was collected (subtract the increase).
questions.explanationJa: 直接法における顧客からの現金回収額は、売上高を売掛金の増減で調整して求める。AR減少 = 収益以上に現金を回収（減少額を加算）。AR増加 = 回収が収益に追いついていない（増加額を減算）。

questions.stem: Under ASC 230, how should changes in restricted cash be presented in the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As investing activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: As financing activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: Included in the beginning and ending cash balances with a reconciliation provided

choices.label: D
choices.text: Excluded from the statement of cash flows entirely

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-18 (codified in ASC 230-10-45-4) requires that restricted cash and restricted cash equivalents be included with cash and cash equivalents when reconciling the beginning and ending totals on the statement of cash flows. A reconciliation to the balance sheet amounts is required either on the face or in the notes.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-18により、CF計算書の期首・期末残高の調整では、拘束性預金および拘束性現金同等物を現金及び現金同等物に含める。B/Sとの金額調整は計算書の本体または注記で行う。

questions.stem: A company recognized $50,000 of stock-based compensation expense during the year. How does this affect the operating section under the indirect method?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Added back to net income as a non-cash expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: Subtracted from net income

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reported as a financing outflow

choices.label: D
choices.text: No adjustment needed

questions.explanation: Stock-based compensation expense is a non-cash charge (no cash was paid to employees for this expense). Under the indirect method, non-cash expenses are added back to net income to compute cash from operating activities, similar to depreciation and amortization.
questions.explanationJa: 株式報酬費用は現金支出を伴わない非現金費用である。間接法では減価償却費と同様に純利益に加算して営業活動CFを算出する。

questions.stem: Entity M paid $30,000 in debt issuance costs when issuing long-term bonds. How should these costs be classified on the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Operating activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: Investing activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: Financing activities

choices.label: D
choices.text: Noncash disclosure

questions.explanation: Debt issuance costs are directly attributable to the issuance of debt and are classified as financing activities on the statement of cash flows. They are typically netted against the proceeds from the debt issuance or shown separately as a financing outflow.
questions.explanationJa: 社債発行費用は負債発行に直接関連するコストであり、CF計算書上は財務活動に分類する。通常、社債発行収入と相殺表示するか、財務活動の流出として別途表示する。

questions.stem: Company A reported net income of $350,000. Additional data: depreciation $40,000; amortization of patent $10,000; loss on disposal of equipment $8,000; increase in accounts receivable $22,000; decrease in inventory $14,000; increase in prepaid expenses $6,000; decrease in accounts payable $18,000; increase in accrued liabilities $9,000; gain on sale of investment $12,000. What is net cash provided by operating activities?
questions.explanation: Net income $350,000 + depreciation $40,000 + amortization $10,000 + loss on disposal $8,000 - gain on sale $12,000 - increase in AR $22,000 + decrease in inventory $14,000 - increase in prepaid $6,000 - decrease in AP $18,000 + increase in accrued liabilities $9,000 = $373,000. Non-cash items are added back, gains subtracted, losses added. Working capital changes: decreases in current assets add, increases subtract; increases in current liabilities add, decreases subtract.
questions.explanationJa: 純利益$350,000 + 減価償却費$40,000 + 特許権償却$10,000 + 設備処分損$8,000 - 投資売却益$12,000 - 売掛金増加$22,000 + 棚卸資産減少$14,000 - 前払費用増加$6,000 - 買掛金減少$18,000 + 未払費用増加$9,000 = $373,000。非現金項目は加算、利益は減算、損失は加算する。運転資本の変動：流動資産の減少は加算、増加は減算。流動負債の増加は加算、減少は減算。

questions.stem: Entity B has the following data: Sales revenue $800,000; beginning AR $65,000; ending AR $78,000; write-offs of uncollectible accounts $5,000; bad debt expense $9,000. Under the direct method, what is cash collected from customers?
questions.explanation: Cash collected from customers = Sales - Increase in gross AR. Gross AR change: ending gross AR - beginning gross AR. Beginning gross AR = $65,000 + allowance; ending gross AR = $78,000 + allowance. However, the net change in AR (net) from $65,000 to $78,000 includes the effect of bad debt expense and write-offs. Direct calculation: Cash collected = Sales - (Ending AR - Beginning AR) - write-offs excluded from AR change = $800,000 - ($78,000 - $65,000) - $5,000 = $800,000 - $13,000 - $5,000 = $782,000. Alternatively: Beginning AR + Sales - Write-offs - Cash collected = Ending AR; $65,000 + $800,000 - $5,000 - Cash = $78,000; Cash = $782,000.
questions.explanationJa: 顧客からの現金回収額 = 期首AR + 売上高 - 貸倒償却 - 期末AR。$65,000 + $800,000 - $5,000 - $78,000 = $782,000。ARのT勘定分析：期首$65,000に売上$800,000を加算し、貸倒償却$5,000と現金回収額を減算すると期末$78,000になる。貸倒引当金繰入額$9,000はARの純額に影響するが、現金の動きには影響しない。

questions.stem: Company C reports: Cost of goods sold $520,000; beginning inventory $90,000; ending inventory $75,000; beginning accounts payable $48,000; ending accounts payable $55,000. Under the direct method, what is cash paid to suppliers?
questions.explanation: Step 1: Purchases = COGS + Ending inventory - Beginning inventory = $520,000 + $75,000 - $90,000 = $505,000. Step 2: Cash paid to suppliers = Purchases - Increase in AP = $505,000 - ($55,000 - $48,000) = $505,000 - $7,000 = $498,000. The decrease in inventory means fewer purchases were needed, and the increase in AP means less cash was paid.
questions.explanationJa: ステップ1：仕入高 = COGS + 期末棚卸資産 - 期首棚卸資産 = $520,000 + $75,000 - $90,000 = $505,000。ステップ2：仕入先への現金支払額 = 仕入高 - AP増加額 = $505,000 - ($55,000 - $48,000) = $505,000 - $7,000 = $498,000。棚卸資産の減少は仕入の減少を意味し、APの増加は支払現金の減少を意味する。

questions.stem: Entity D acquired a building for $500,000 by paying $150,000 cash and signing a mortgage note for $350,000. Entity D also purchased equipment for $80,000 cash and sold old machinery (cost $120,000, accumulated depreciation $90,000) for $40,000 cash. What is net cash used in investing activities?
questions.explanation: Building purchase (cash portion only): -$150,000 (the $350,000 mortgage is a noncash transaction disclosed separately). Equipment purchase: -$80,000. Sale of machinery: +$40,000. Net investing: -$150,000 - $80,000 + $40,000 = -$190,000. The noncash portion ($350,000 note for building) is excluded from the CF statement and disclosed as a noncash investing/financing activity.
questions.explanationJa: 建物購入（現金部分のみ）：-$150,000（$350,000の住宅ローンは非現金取引として別途開示）。設備購入：-$80,000。機械売却：+$40,000。投資活動純額：-$150,000 - $80,000 + $40,000 = -$190,000。非現金部分（建物取得のための$350,000の手形）はCF計算書から除外され、非現金の投資・財務活動として開示される。

questions.stem: Company E reported net income of $180,000. During the year, the company declared and paid dividends of $40,000, repurchased treasury stock for $60,000, issued bonds at a premium receiving $530,000 (face $500,000), and recognized amortization of bond premium of $3,000. Depreciation was $35,000 and there was a $10,000 unrealized loss on trading securities. What is cash from operating activities?
questions.explanation: Cash from operations starts with net income $180,000. Add back: depreciation $35,000 (non-cash expense); unrealized loss on trading securities $10,000 (non-cash mark-to-market). Subtract: amortization of bond premium $3,000 (premium amortization reduces interest expense below cash interest paid). Dividends, treasury stock, and bond issuance are all financing activities. Cash from operations = $180,000 + $35,000 + $10,000 - $3,000 = $222,000.
questions.explanationJa: 営業活動CFは純利益$180,000から出発。加算：減価償却費$35,000（非現金費用）、売買目的有価証券の未実現損失$10,000（非現金の時価評価）。減算：社債プレミアム償却$3,000（プレミアム償却により利息費用が現金利息支払額より少なく計上されるため）。配当、自己株式取得、社債発行は全て財務活動。営業活動CF = $180,000 + $35,000 + $10,000 - $3,000 = $222,000。

questions.stem: During Year 1, Company F had the following transactions: issued 10,000 shares of common stock ($1 par) at $15 per share; paid cash dividends of $25,000; repaid a $100,000 long-term note; borrowed $200,000 on a new long-term note; paid $8,000 in debt issuance costs related to the new note. What is net cash provided by financing activities?
questions.explanation: Stock issuance: 10,000 × $15 = +$150,000. Dividends paid: -$25,000. Repayment of note: -$100,000. New borrowing: +$200,000. Debt issuance costs: -$8,000. Net financing: $150,000 - $25,000 - $100,000 + $200,000 - $8,000 = $217,000. All items are financing activities. Debt issuance costs are classified as financing per ASC 230.

questions.stem: Entity G reports: Cash from operating activities $420,000; capital expenditures $180,000; proceeds from asset sales $30,000; dividends paid $50,000; debt repayments $75,000; interest paid $40,000 (included in operating). What is Entity G's free cash flow (defined as operating cash flow less capital expenditures)?
questions.explanation: Free cash flow (basic definition) = Cash from operating activities - Capital expenditures = $420,000 - $180,000 = $240,000. Proceeds from asset sales, dividends, debt repayments are not subtracted under the basic FCF definition. Interest is already included in operating cash flow under U.S. GAAP so no further adjustment is needed.
questions.explanationJa: フリー・キャッシュ・フロー（基本定義）= 営業活動CF - 資本的支出 = $420,000 - $180,000 = $240,000。資産売却収入、配当、借入金返済は基本的なFCF定義では差し引かない。利息は米国GAAPに基づき営業活動CFに既に含まれているため、追加の調整は不要。

questions.stem: Company H acquired 100% of Target Inc. for $600,000 cash. Target's assets at fair value: cash $45,000, AR $80,000, inventory $60,000, equipment $300,000, goodwill $200,000. Liabilities assumed: AP $50,000, notes payable $35,000. On Company H's consolidated statement of cash flows, what amount is reported as cash used in investing activities for this acquisition?
questions.explanation: When reporting a business combination on the cash flow statement, the cash paid is shown net of cash acquired. Cash used in investing = Total cash paid - Cash acquired from Target = $600,000 - $45,000 = $555,000. Per ASC 230-10-45-21, the cash paid for an acquisition is investing, net of cash obtained.
questions.explanationJa: 企業結合をキャッシュ・フロー計算書に報告する際、支払現金は取得した現金と相殺して表示する。投資活動のキャッシュ使用額 = 支払総額 - 取得先の現金 = $600,000 - $45,000 = $555,000。ASC 230-10-45-21に基づき、企業買収のための現金支払は、取得した現金を控除して投資活動に表示する。

questions.stem: Entity J reported income from continuing operations of $250,000, which includes: depreciation $45,000; equity method income from an investee of $30,000 (dividends received $18,000); deferred tax expense $12,000; increase in AR $20,000; decrease in AP $15,000; increase in unearned revenue $25,000. What is cash provided by operating activities?
questions.explanation: Start with income $250,000. Add: depreciation $45,000; deferred tax expense $12,000 (non-cash portion of tax); increase in unearned revenue $25,000 (liability increase). Subtract: equity income in excess of dividends ($30,000 - $18,000 = $12,000 subtracted, since only dividends represent cash); increase in AR $20,000; decrease in AP $15,000. Total: $250,000 + $45,000 + $12,000 + $25,000 - $12,000 - $20,000 - $15,000 = $285,000.

questions.stem: Company K declared total dividends of $35,000 during the year, of which $8,000 remains unpaid as dividends payable at year-end. Beginning dividends payable was $0. No other financing activities occurred. What amount is reported as cash used in financing activities?
questions.explanation: Only cash dividends actually paid are reported on the statement of cash flows. Dividends paid = Dividends declared - Increase in dividends payable = $35,000 - ($8,000 - $0) = $27,000. The $8,000 unpaid portion will appear on the cash flow statement when paid in a future period. The declaration of a dividend is not a cash flow event.
questions.explanationJa: キャッシュ・フロー計算書には実際に支払った配当のみを計上する。支払配当 = 宣言配当 - 未払配当の増加 = $35,000 - ($8,000 - $0) = $27,000。未払分$8,000は将来の支払時に計上される。配当の宣言自体は現金の動きを伴わないためCFには影響しない。

questions.stem: Under US GAAP (ASC 230), how are cash payments for interest classified on the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Operating activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: Financing activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: Investing activities

choices.label: D
choices.text: Either operating or financing, at the entity's election

questions.explanation: Under US GAAP (ASC 230-10-45-17), interest paid is classified as an operating activity. This differs from IFRS (IAS 7), which allows interest paid to be classified as either operating or financing activities. The rationale is that interest expense enters into the determination of net income.
questions.explanationJa: US GAAP（ASC 230-10-45-17）では、支払利息は営業活動に分類する。これはIFRS（IAS 7）が支払利息を営業活動または財務活動のいずれかに分類することを認めるのとは異なる。利息費用が純利益の決定に含まれることが理由である。

questions.stem: Entity A pays income taxes of $300,000 during the year, of which $50,000 relates to a gain on the sale of equipment. Under US GAAP, how should the $300,000 be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $250,000 operating; $50,000 investing

choices.label: B
choices.text: $300,000 entirely in operating activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: $300,000 entirely in financing activities

choices.label: D
choices.text: $250,000 operating; $50,000 financing

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-17, income tax payments are classified as operating activities, regardless of the nature of the transaction that generated the tax. Even though $50,000 relates to an investing transaction (equipment sale), the entire $300,000 is classified as operating. This is a practical expedient since allocating taxes to specific activities would be impracticable.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-17により、法人税の支払いは税金を生じさせた取引の性質にかかわらず営業活動に分類する。$50,000が投資取引（設備売却）に関連していても、$300,000全額が営業活動に分類される。税金を特定の活動に配分することは実務上困難であるためである。

questions.stem: Entity B receives dividends of $80,000 from an equity method investee. Under US GAAP, how should these dividends be classified on Entity B's statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Operating activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: Investing activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: Operating or investing, depending on the nature of the distribution

choices.label: D
choices.text: Financing activities

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-21A (ASU 2016-15), an entity may elect to classify distributions from equity method investees using either (1) the cumulative earnings approach (classify as operating until cumulative distributions exceed cumulative equity earnings) or (2) the nature of the distribution approach (based on the nature of the investee's activity). This is an accounting policy election.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-21A（ASU 2016-15）により、持分法投資先からの分配は(1) 累積利益アプローチ（累積分配が累積持分法利益を超えるまで営業活動に分類）または(2) 分配の性質アプローチ（投資先の活動の性質に基づく）のいずれかで分類する会計方針の選択ができる。

questions.stem: Entity C receives $500,000 from an insurance company for a building destroyed by fire. The building had a carrying value of $350,000. Under ASU 2016-15, how should the insurance proceeds be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $350,000 investing; $150,000 operating

choices.label: B
choices.text: $500,000 investing

choices.label: C
choices.text: $500,000 operating

choices.label: D
choices.text: Based on the nature of the loss event and the related insurance claim

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-21B (ASU 2016-15), insurance proceeds should be classified on the basis of the nature of the loss event. For insurance proceeds from the destruction of a building (a long-lived asset), the proceeds would be classified as investing activities. If the proceeds were from a business interruption claim, they would be operating activities.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-21B（ASU 2016-15）により、保険金は損失事象の性質に基づき分類する。建物（長期性資産）の滅失による保険金は投資活動に分類。事業中断による保険金は営業活動に分類する。

questions.subtopic: Factoring of Receivables - Cash Flow Impact
questions.stem: Entity D factors $200,000 of accounts receivable without recourse and receives $185,000 (after a $15,000 factoring fee). The transfer qualifies as a sale under ASC 860. How should the $185,000 be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Operating activity

choices.label: B
choices.text: Investing activity

choices.label: C
choices.text: Financing activity

choices.label: D
choices.text: Not reported on the cash flow statement

questions.explanation: When receivables are factored in a transaction that qualifies as a sale (derecognition under ASC 860), the cash received is classified as an operating activity because it relates to the collection of trade receivables. The $15,000 loss on sale is also an operating item. If the transfer did not qualify as a sale, proceeds would be financing (a secured borrowing).
questions.explanationJa: 売掛金のファクタリングがASC 860に基づく売却（認識の中止）に該当する場合、受取現金は売掛金の回収に関連するため営業活動に分類する。$15,000の売却損も営業項目。売却に該当しない場合、受取額は財務活動（担保付借入）に分類される。

questions.stem: Entity E makes a finance lease payment of $50,000, of which $15,000 is interest and $35,000 is principal reduction. How should this payment be classified on the cash flow statement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $50,000 in financing activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: $15,000 in operating activities; $35,000 in financing activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: $50,000 in operating activities

choices.label: D
choices.text: $15,000 in financing activities; $35,000 in operating activities

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-45-5, for a finance lease, the interest portion of the payment ($15,000) is classified as operating (consistent with interest paid classification under US GAAP), and the principal portion ($35,000) is classified as financing. For operating leases, all cash payments are operating activities.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-45-5により、ファイナンスリースでは支払いの利息部分($15,000)は営業活動（US GAAPの支払利息の分類と整合）に、元本部分($35,000)は財務活動に分類する。オペレーティング・リースでは全ての現金支払が営業活動となる。

questions.stem: Entity F acquires Entity G for $8,000,000 cash. At acquisition, Entity G has cash of $1,200,000 and a bank loan of $3,000,000 that Entity F assumes. What is the net cash outflow from investing activities?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-21, the acquisition is reported as an investing activity, net of cash acquired. Cash paid = $8,000,000. Cash acquired = $1,200,000. Net investing outflow = $8,000,000 − $1,200,000 = $6,800,000. The assumed debt is a non-cash financing/investing activity disclosed in supplemental information.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-21により、企業取得は取得した現金を控除して投資活動として報告する。支払現金 = $8,000,000。取得現金 = $1,200,000。投資活動の正味流出 = $8,000,000 − $1,200,000 = $6,800,000。引き受けた債務は補足情報で開示する非現金の財務/投資活動である。

questions.stem: Under US GAAP, which of the following is true regarding cash flow per share?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Cash flow per share must be disclosed on the face of the cash flow statement

choices.label: B
choices.text: Cash flow per share may be disclosed in the footnotes

choices.label: C
choices.text: Cash flow per share is prohibited from being reported

choices.label: D
choices.text: Cash flow per share is required only for public companies

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-3, entities are prohibited from reporting cash flow per share amounts. The FASB believes that reporting cash flow per share could mislead users by implying that cash flow is an appropriate measure of performance, potentially diverting attention from earnings per share.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-3により、1株当たりキャッシュ・フローの開示は禁止されている。FASBはキャッシュ・フローが業績の適切な指標であるとの誤解を与え、1株当たり利益から注意をそらす可能性があると考えている。

questions.stem: Which of the following must be disclosed as supplemental information to the statement of cash flows under US GAAP?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Total amount of cash and cash equivalents at the end of the period

choices.label: B
choices.text: Cash paid for interest and income taxes during the period

choices.label: C
choices.text: Total depreciation and amortization expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: Details of all investing activities

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-50-2, entities must disclose the amount of cash paid for interest (net of amounts capitalized) and income taxes during the period. These supplemental disclosures are required whether the entity uses the direct or indirect method. This helps users understand the cash impact of these significant expenses.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-50-2により、期間中に支払った利息（資産計上額控除後）と法人税の金額を開示しなければならない。これらの補足開示は直接法・間接法のいずれを使用する場合でも必要である。利用者がこれら重要な費用の現金影響を理解するのに役立つ。

questions.stem: Entity H enters into an interest rate swap designated as a cash flow hedge. During the year, Entity H receives net settlements of $40,000 from the swap. How should the settlements be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Operating activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: Investing activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: Financing activities

choices.label: D
choices.text: Same classification as the hedged item

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-27, cash flows from derivative instruments that are designated and qualify as hedges are classified in the same category as the cash flows of the hedged item. If the swap hedges interest on debt (operating), the settlements are operating. If it hedges a forecasted purchase (operating or investing), it follows that classification.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-27により、ヘッジとして指定され適格なデリバティブのキャッシュ・フローはヘッジ対象項目のキャッシュ・フローと同じ区分に分類する。スワップが債務の利息（営業）をヘッジする場合、決済額は営業。予定購入（営業または投資）をヘッジする場合、その分類に従う。

questions.stem: Entity J purchases 90-day Treasury bills for $500,000. Under ASC 230, how should this purchase be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Investing activity

choices.label: B
choices.text: No classification needed — it is a cash equivalent

choices.label: C
choices.text: Financing activity

choices.label: D
choices.text: Operating activity

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-20 (Glossary), cash equivalents are short-term, highly liquid investments with original maturities of three months or less. Treasury bills with 90-day maturity qualify as cash equivalents. Purchases and sales of cash equivalents are not reported as investing activities but are included in the cash and cash equivalents balance.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-20（用語集）により、現金同等物は当初満期が3ヶ月以内の短期・高流動性投資である。満期90日の財務省短期証券は現金同等物に該当する。現金同等物の購入・売却は投資活動として報告せず、現金及び現金同等物残高に含める。

questions.stem: Entity K acquired a business and recorded contingent consideration of $1,000,000 at the acquisition date. In Year 2, Entity K pays $1,200,000 to settle the contingent consideration. Under ASU 2016-15, how should the payment be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $1,200,000 in financing activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: $1,000,000 in financing activities; $200,000 in operating activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: $1,200,000 in operating activities

choices.label: D
choices.text: $1,000,000 in investing activities; $200,000 in operating activities

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-21C (ASU 2016-15), payments of contingent consideration made after a business combination are classified as financing up to the amount recognized at acquisition ($1,000,000), and any excess ($200,000) is classified as operating. This ensures consistent classification regardless of when the contingency is resolved.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-45-21C（ASU 2016-15）により、企業結合後の条件付対価の支払いは取得日に認識した金額（$1,000,000）まで財務活動に、超過額（$200,000）は営業活動に分類する。偶発事象がいつ解決されるかにかかわらず一貫した分類を確保する。

questions.stem: Entity L reports equity method income of $120,000 from its 30% investment in Entity M. Entity L received dividends of $45,000 from Entity M. Using the indirect method, what adjustment is made to net income in the operating section?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Subtract $120,000 and add $45,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Subtract $75,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Subtracting equity income and adding dividends received ($120,000 − $45,000 = −$75,000 net) produces the same result as subtracting $75,000 directly

questions.explanation: Under the indirect method, equity method income ($120,000) is subtracted from net income because it exceeds the cash received. Dividends received ($45,000) are added back as they represent actual cash inflows. The net adjustment is a subtraction of $75,000 ($120,000 − $45,000). Both approaches yield the same result.
questions.explanationJa: 間接法では、持分法利益($120,000)は受取現金を上回るため純利益から減算する。受取配当($45,000)は実際の現金流入を表すため加算する。正味調整は$75,000の減算($120,000 − $45,000)。いずれのアプローチでも同じ結果となる。

questions.stem: Entity N converts $2,000,000 of convertible bonds into 50,000 shares of common stock. No cash is exchanged. How should this be reported on the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a financing activity — retirement of debt

choices.label: B
choices.text: As a financing activity — issuance of stock

choices.label: C
choices.text: Not on the statement of cash flows, but disclosed as a significant noncash investing and financing activity

choices.label: D
choices.text: As both investing and financing activities

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-50-3, noncash investing and financing transactions (such as conversion of debt to equity) are excluded from the statement of cash flows but must be disclosed either in a supplemental schedule or in the notes to the financial statements. The conversion involves no cash and thus does not appear on the cash flow statement.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 230-10-50-3により、非現金の投資・財務活動（社債の株式への転換等）はキャッシュ・フロー計算書から除外されるが、補足スケジュールまたは財務諸表の注記で開示しなければならない。転換は現金を伴わないためキャッシュ・フロー計算書には表示されない。

questions.stem: Entity P has a cash flow hedge on a forecasted purchase. During Year 1, $30,000 of gain is recognized in OCI. In Year 2, the hedged purchase occurs and $30,000 is reclassified from OCI to cost of goods sold. Using the indirect method, what is the Year 2 operating cash flow adjustment for the reclassification?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Add $30,000 to net income in operating activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: No adjustment needed — the reclassification affects net income and is already in operations

choices.label: C
choices.text: Subtract $30,000 from net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Report $30,000 in investing activities

questions.explanation: When the reclassification from OCI to earnings occurs (e.g., as part of COGS), it is already reflected in net income. Under the indirect method, no additional adjustment is needed for the reclassification itself. The cash settlement of the hedge was classified when the settlement occurred, and the reclassification is a non-cash adjustment within the income statement.
questions.explanationJa: OCIから損益への再分類（例：売上原価の一部として）が行われた場合、既に純利益に反映されている。間接法では再分類自体について追加の調整は不要。ヘッジの現金決済は決済時に分類済みであり、再分類は損益計算書内の非現金の調整である。

questions.subtopic: Operating to Free Cash Flow Reconciliation
questions.stem: Entity Q has cash from operating activities of $800,000, capital expenditures of $200,000, proceeds from sale of equipment of $50,000, and dividends paid of $100,000. What is Entity Q's free cash flow (FCF)?
questions.explanation: Free cash flow is a non-GAAP measure commonly calculated as: Operating cash flow − Capital expenditures. FCF = $800,000 − $200,000 = $600,000. Some analysts include proceeds from asset sales or subtract dividends, but the most common definition is operating cash flow minus capex. FCF is not defined under GAAP and must be reconciled to the nearest GAAP measure.
questions.explanationJa: フリーキャッシュフローは非GAAP指標で一般に営業キャッシュフロー − 資本的支出で計算する。FCF = $800,000 − $200,000 = $600,000。資産売却収入の加算や配当の控除を含むアナリストもいるが、最も一般的な定義は営業CF − 設備投資。FCFはGAAPで定義されず、最も近いGAAP指標との調整表示が必要。

questions.stem: Entity R securitizes $5,000,000 of receivables through a special purpose entity and receives $4,800,000 in cash. The transfer qualifies as a sale under ASC 860. Entity R retains a servicing asset of $100,000 and a subordinated interest of $200,000. How is the $4,800,000 classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Operating activities — it represents collection of receivables

choices.label: B
choices.text: Investing activities — sale of financial assets

choices.label: C
choices.text: Financing activities — it is a borrowing

choices.label: D
choices.text: Split between operating and investing activities

questions.explanation: When a securitization qualifies as a sale under ASC 860, the cash received from the transfer of receivables that originated from operating activities (such as trade receivables) is classified as operating activities. The servicing asset and retained interest are non-cash items. If the receivables were originated as investing activities, the cash would be classified as investing.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 860に基づく売却に該当する場合、営業活動から生じた売掛金の譲渡による受取現金は営業活動に分類する。サービシング資産と劣後持分は非現金項目。売掛金が投資活動として組成された場合、現金は投資活動に分類される。

questions.stem: Entity S's balance sheet shows: Accounts Receivable decreased by $25,000; Inventory increased by $40,000; Accounts Payable increased by $15,000; Accrued Liabilities decreased by $10,000. Using the indirect method, what is the net working capital adjustment to operating cash flows?
questions.explanation: AR decrease: +$25,000 (cash collected exceeded revenue). Inventory increase: −$40,000 (cash spent on inventory exceeded COGS). AP increase: +$15,000 (expenses incurred but not yet paid). Accrued liabilities decrease: −$10,000 (paid more than incurred). Net adjustment = $25,000 − $40,000 + $15,000 − $10,000 = −$10,000.
questions.explanationJa: AR減少：+$25,000（現金回収が収益を上回る）。棚卸資産増加：−$40,000（棚卸資産の現金支出がCOGSを上回る）。AP増加：+$15,000（発生したが未払い）。未払費用減少：−$10,000（発生額を超えて支払い）。正味調整 = $25,000 − $40,000 + $15,000 − $10,000 = −$10,000。

questions.stem: Entity T has a foreign subsidiary with a functional currency of the euro. The subsidiary's cash balance is €1,000,000. The exchange rate was $1.10 at the beginning of the year and $1.15 at year-end. What is the effect of exchange rate changes on cash?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $50,000 increase in cash

choices.label: B
choices.text: $50,000 decrease in cash

choices.label: C
choices.text: $0 — exchange rate effects are not reported

choices.label: D
choices.text: $50,000 reported as OCI

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-230-45-1, the effect of exchange rate changes on cash and cash equivalents is reported as a separate line item on the statement of cash flows. The subsidiary's cash = €1,000,000 × ($1.15 − $1.10) = $50,000 increase. This is shown separately from operating, investing, and financing activities.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 830-230-45-1により、為替レート変動が現金及び現金同等物に与える影響はキャッシュ・フロー計算書で別個の項目として報告する。子会社の現金 = €1,000,000 × ($1.15 − $1.10) = $50,000の増加。これは営業・投資・財務活動とは別に表示する。

questions.stem: Entity U receives a $1,000,000 government grant for the purchase of pollution control equipment. Under US GAAP (IAS 20 is not applicable), how should the grant receipt be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Operating activities as grant income

choices.label: B
choices.text: Investing activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: Financing activities

choices.label: D
choices.text: Based on the nature of the grant — in this case, investing activities since it relates to equipment purchase

questions.explanation: Under US GAAP, there is no specific comprehensive standard for government grants (unlike IFRS/IAS 20). However, ASU 2021-10 requires disclosures. For cash flow classification, the grant proceeds should be classified based on the nature of the activity — since this grant is for equipment purchase (a capital expenditure), it would be classified as investing activities.
questions.explanationJa: US GAAPには政府補助金に関する包括的な基準がない（IFRS/IAS 20とは異なる）。ただしASU 2021-10で開示が要求される。キャッシュ・フロー分類では、補助金の受取額は活動の性質に基づき分類する。この補助金は設備購入（資本的支出）のためであり、投資活動に分類される。

questions.stem: Under U.S. GAAP (ASC 230), how is cash paid for interest classified on the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Operating activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: Financing activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: Investing activities

choices.label: D
choices.text: Either operating or financing, at the entity's election

questions.explanation: Under U.S. GAAP (ASC 230-10-45-17), cash paid for interest is classified as an operating activity. This differs from IFRS, which permits interest paid to be classified as either operating or financing. Under U.S. GAAP, the classification is mandatory—there is no election. Cash paid for interest must also be disclosed as supplemental information when using the indirect method.
questions.explanationJa: US GAAPでは利息の支払いは営業活動に分類される（選択の余地なし）。IFRSでは営業または財務活動の選択が認められるが、US GAAPでは強制的に営業活動となる。間接法使用時には補足情報として開示が必要。

questions.stem: Hart Corp. reported net income of $500,000. During the year, depreciation expense was $80,000, accounts receivable increased by $30,000, and accounts payable decreased by $20,000. Using the indirect method, what is cash provided by operating activities?
questions.explanation: Indirect method starts with net income and adjusts for non-cash items and working capital changes: $500,000 + $80,000 (add back depreciation) − $30,000 (increase in A/R reduces cash) − $20,000 (decrease in A/P reduces cash) = $530,000. An increase in a current asset is a use of cash; a decrease in a current liability is also a use of cash.
questions.explanationJa: 間接法：純利益$500,000＋減価償却費$80,000（加算）−売掛金増加$30,000（減算）−買掛金減少$20,000（減算）＝$530,000。流動資産の増加と流動負債の減少は資金の使用を意味する。

questions.stem: Lee Co. sold equipment with a book value of $120,000 for $145,000 cash. Where is the $145,000 cash proceeds reported on the statement of cash flows, and how is the $25,000 gain treated under the indirect method?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $145,000 as investing inflow; deduct the $25,000 gain from operating activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: $120,000 as investing inflow and $25,000 as operating inflow

choices.label: C
choices.text: $145,000 as operating inflow; no adjustment needed

choices.label: D
choices.text: $145,000 as investing inflow; add the $25,000 gain to operating activities

questions.explanation: The full cash proceeds of $145,000 are reported as an investing activity inflow (ASC 230-10-45-12). Under the indirect method, the $25,000 gain is deducted from net income in the operating section to avoid double-counting—it is already included in net income but belongs in investing, not operating. The gain is not a separate cash flow; only the total proceeds are.
questions.explanationJa: 売却代金$145,000全額を投資活動のキャッシュ・インフローとして報告。間接法では$25,000の利益を営業活動から控除する（純利益に含まれているが、投資活動に属するため二重計上を回避）。

questions.stem: Under U.S. GAAP, how are cash dividends received from an equity method investee classified on the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Operating activities, unless they represent a return of investment (liquidating dividends), which are investing

choices.label: B
choices.text: Always investing activities since they relate to an investment

choices.label: C
choices.text: Always operating activities regardless of the nature

choices.label: D
choices.text: Financing activities as a return to shareholders

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-21, dividends received from equity method investees are generally classified as operating activities because they represent a return on investment (analogous to interest income). However, cumulative distributions received in excess of the investor's cumulative equity in earnings since the date of investment (i.e., returns of investment or liquidating dividends) are classified as investing activities.
questions.explanationJa: 持分法適用投資先からの受取配当金は、原則として営業活動に分類される（投資収益の回収）。ただし、投資日以降の累積持分利益を超える累積配当額（投資の回収・清算配当）は投資活動に分類される。

questions.stem: Wynn Corp. acquired a building by issuing a $2,000,000 mortgage note directly to the seller. How should this transaction be reported on the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Disclosed as a noncash investing and financing activity in a supplemental schedule

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reported as a $2,000,000 investing outflow and a $2,000,000 financing inflow

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reported only as a $2,000,000 financing inflow

choices.label: D
choices.text: Not reported on the statement of cash flows or in supplemental disclosures

questions.explanation: Noncash investing and financing activities that do not involve cash flows are excluded from the body of the cash flow statement but must be disclosed in a supplemental schedule or narrative (ASC 230-10-50-3 through 50-6). The acquisition of an asset by assuming a liability directly is a noncash transaction. It is not reported as if two separate cash flows occurred.
questions.explanationJa: 現金の収支を伴わない投資・財務活動は、キャッシュ・フロー計算書の本体には含めず、補足スケジュールで開示する。建物の取得と引き換えの住宅ローン発行は非現金取引であり、2つの独立したキャッシュ・フローとして報告しない。

questions.stem: Under ASU 2016-18, how should changes in restricted cash be presented on the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Restricted cash is included in the beginning and ending cash balances; transfers between cash and restricted cash are not shown as separate line items

choices.label: B
choices.text: Changes in restricted cash are shown as investing activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: Changes in restricted cash are shown as financing activities

choices.label: D
choices.text: Restricted cash is excluded from the reconciliation of cash and only disclosed in notes

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-18 (ASC 230-10-45-4) requires that the statement of cash flows explain the change in the total of cash, cash equivalents, and restricted cash. Restricted cash is included in both the beginning and ending balances. Transfers between cash and restricted cash are no longer presented as separate investing or financing activities—they are eliminated from the statement because both are included in the total.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-18により、制限付き現金はキャッシュ・フロー計算書の期首・期末残高に含める。現金と制限付き現金間の振替は、両方が合計に含まれるため独立した項目として表示しない。

questions.stem: Under U.S. GAAP, how are cash payments for income taxes classified on the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Operating activities, unless specifically identifiable with investing or financing activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: Always operating activities without exception

choices.label: C
choices.text: Allocated among operating, investing, and financing based on a proration formula

choices.label: D
choices.text: Financing activities because taxes are a government obligation

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-17, income tax payments are generally classified as operating activities. However, ASC 230-10-45-17A (per ASU 2016-15) clarifies that if the tax payment is directly attributable to a specific investing or financing activity (such as tax on a gain from sale of a business), it may be classified accordingly. The total amount of income taxes paid must be disclosed.
questions.explanationJa: 法人税の支払いは原則として営業活動に分類される。ただし、特定の投資・財務活動に直接帰属できる場合（事業売却益に対する税金など）は、その活動に分類できる。支払法人税額の総額は開示が必要。

questions.stem: Blake Corp. issues bonds and pays $150,000 in debt issuance costs. How should the $150,000 be classified on the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Financing activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: Operating activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: Investing activities

choices.label: D
choices.text: Deducted from the bond proceeds as a noncash item

questions.explanation: Debt issuance costs are directly related to the issuance of debt and are classified as financing activities on the statement of cash flows (ASC 230-10-45-15). They are typically presented as a separate financing outflow or as a reduction of the bond proceeds inflow. The costs are not operating expenses for cash flow purposes even though they may be amortized to interest expense over the life of the debt.
questions.explanationJa: 社債発行費は債務の発行に直接関連するため、財務活動に分類される。社債発行代金とは別の財務活動の支出として、または社債収入からの控除として表示される。損益計算書上は支払利息として償却されるが、キャッシュ・フロー上は財務活動である。

questions.stem: Under the direct method of presenting operating activities, which of the following is reported as a separate line item?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Cash received from customers

choices.label: B
choices.text: Depreciation expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: Gain on sale of equipment

choices.label: D
choices.text: Increase in accounts receivable

questions.explanation: The direct method (ASC 230-10-45-25) presents major classes of gross cash receipts and payments in the operating section. Cash received from customers is a primary line item. Depreciation is a non-cash charge (not shown under direct method). Gain on sale of equipment is an investing-related item. Changes in accounts receivable are adjustments shown under the indirect method, not the direct method.
questions.explanationJa: 直接法では営業活動の主要な現金収入・支出の総額を表示する。「顧客からの現金受取額」が主要項目。減価償却費は非現金項目のため直接法では表示しない。売掛金の増減は間接法の調整項目である。

questions.stem: A fire destroys Dorr Corp.'s warehouse (book value $800,000). Dorr receives insurance proceeds of $1,200,000. Under ASU 2016-15, how should the insurance proceeds be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Investing activities for the amount that is a recovery of the asset ($800,000) and operating or investing for the excess ($400,000)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Entirely as operating activities because insurance is an operating expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: Entirely as investing activities

choices.label: D
choices.text: Financing activities because the insurance company is providing funds

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-15 (ASC 230-10-45-21B) clarifies that insurance proceeds received for the destruction of a long-lived asset should be classified based on the nature of the loss event. The portion that represents recovery of the carrying amount of the asset ($800,000) is classified as investing. The excess ($400,000 gain portion) may be classified as investing or operating depending on the entity's policy, but must be consistently applied.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-15により、長期性資産の滅失に対する保険金は、帳簿価額の回収分（$800,000）を投資活動に分類する。超過分（$400,000）は事業体の方針により投資または営業活動に分類するが、一貫した適用が必要。

questions.stem: Finn Corp. factors $500,000 of accounts receivable without recourse for $475,000 cash. The factor retains $25,000 as its fee. How does Finn classify the $475,000 on its cash flow statement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Operating activities because the receivables arise from the sale of goods

choices.label: B
choices.text: Investing activities as a sale of financial assets

choices.label: C
choices.text: Financing activities as a borrowing arrangement

choices.label: D
choices.text: Operating activities for $450,000 and financing for $25,000

questions.explanation: When receivables are factored without recourse and the transaction qualifies as a sale under ASC 860, the cash received is classified as operating activities because the underlying receivables arose from the entity's revenue-generating activities (ASC 230-10-45-16(c)). The factoring fee reduces operating cash receipts. If the factoring were with recourse and treated as a secured borrowing, it would be a financing activity.
questions.explanationJa: リコースなしのファクタリングが売却として適格な場合、受取現金は営業活動に分類される（売掛金が収益活動から生じたため）。リコース付きで担保付借入として扱われる場合は財務活動となる。

questions.stem: Hale Corp. reported net income of $900,000. Stock-based compensation expense for the year was $60,000. No stock options were exercised. Under the indirect method, how is the stock-based compensation treated?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Added back to net income as a non-cash expense in operating activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reported as a financing activity outflow

choices.label: C
choices.text: No adjustment needed since it is already reflected in net income

choices.label: D
choices.text: Subtracted from net income in operating activities

questions.explanation: Stock-based compensation expense is a non-cash charge that reduces net income but does not use cash. Under the indirect method (ASC 230-10-45-28), non-cash expenses are added back to net income to arrive at cash from operations. The $60,000 is added back to the $900,000 net income. Cash flow effects from stock compensation only arise when options are actually exercised.
questions.explanationJa: 株式報酬費用は純利益を減少させるが現金支出を伴わない非現金費用であるため、間接法では純利益に加算する。$60,000を$900,000の純利益に加算する。現金効果はオプションが実際に行使された時にのみ生じる。

questions.stem: Mead Inc. makes a $50,000 payment on its finance lease obligation, of which $12,000 represents interest and $38,000 represents principal reduction. How should this payment be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $12,000 as operating activities and $38,000 as financing activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: Entire $50,000 as financing activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: Entire $50,000 as operating activities

choices.label: D
choices.text: $38,000 as investing activities and $12,000 as operating activities

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-45-5, for a finance lease, the interest portion of the lease payment is classified as operating activities (consistent with the treatment of interest on debt), while the principal portion is classified as financing activities. This split classification applies specifically to finance leases. Operating lease payments, by contrast, are typically all classified as operating activities.
questions.explanationJa: ファイナンス・リースの支払いは、利息部分（$12,000）を営業活動に、元本返済部分（$38,000）を財務活動に分類する。オペレーティング・リースの支払いは原則すべて営業活動に分類される点と対照的である。

questions.stem: Platt Corp. acquires 100% of Sark Inc. for $5,000,000 cash. At the acquisition date, Sark has $300,000 of cash on hand. What amount is reported as an investing activity for this acquisition?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $4,700,000 net cash outflow

choices.label: B
choices.text: $5,000,000 cash outflow

choices.label: C
choices.text: $5,300,000 cash outflow

choices.label: D
choices.text: $300,000 cash inflow

questions.explanation: Under ASC 230-10-45-21(d), when a business is acquired for cash, the payment is classified as an investing activity. The cash acquired with the subsidiary ($300,000) offsets the acquisition price, resulting in a net investing outflow of $5,000,000 − $300,000 = $4,700,000. The acquiree's cash becomes part of the consolidated cash balance from the acquisition date.
questions.explanationJa: 企業買収の現金支払いは投資活動に分類される。被取得企業が保有する現金$300,000が相殺され、純投資活動キャッシュ・アウトフローは$5,000,000−$300,000=$4,700,000となる。

questions.stem: Opus Corp. received $200,000 in customer prepayments during the year. By year-end, $150,000 had been recognized as revenue. Under the indirect method, what adjustment is made for the $50,000 increase in deferred revenue?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Added to net income in the operating section

choices.label: B
choices.text: Subtracted from net income in the operating section

choices.label: C
choices.text: Reported as a financing inflow

choices.label: D
choices.text: No adjustment needed

questions.explanation: An increase in deferred revenue (a current liability) means that cash was received but not yet recognized as revenue. Under the indirect method, increases in current liabilities are added to net income because the cash collected exceeded the revenue recognized. The $50,000 increase ($200,000 received − $150,000 recognized) is added to net income to reflect the actual operating cash inflow.
questions.explanationJa: 繰延収益（流動負債）の増加は、現金を受け取ったが収益として認識されていないことを意味する。間接法では流動負債の増加は純利益に加算する。$50,000の増加分を加算して実際の営業キャッシュ・インフローを反映する。

questions.stem: Rowe Corp. converts $3,000,000 of convertible bonds into 150,000 shares of common stock. How is this reported on the statement of cash flows?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a noncash investing and financing activity in the supplemental schedule

choices.label: B
choices.text: $3,000,000 financing outflow for bond retirement and $3,000,000 financing inflow for stock issuance

choices.label: C
choices.text: Only as a financing outflow of $3,000,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Not reported anywhere since it involves only equity accounts

questions.explanation: The conversion of debt to equity is a significant noncash transaction that does not involve any cash flows. Under ASC 230-10-50-3, it must be disclosed in a supplemental schedule of noncash investing and financing activities. It cannot be reported as if two separate cash flows occurred (a retirement and an issuance), because no cash changed hands.
questions.explanationJa: 社債の株式への転換は現金の収支を伴わない重要な非現金取引であり、非現金投資・財務活動の補足スケジュールで開示する。現金が動いていないため、2つの独立したキャッシュ・フローとしては報告しない。

questions.stem: Two years after acquiring a subsidiary, Zane Corp. pays $500,000 of contingent consideration to the former owners. The original acquisition-date fair value of the contingent consideration was $400,000. Under ASU 2016-15, how should the $500,000 payment be classified?

choices.label: A
choices.text: $400,000 as financing activities and $100,000 as operating activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: Entirely as investing activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: Entirely as financing activities

choices.label: D
choices.text: $400,000 as investing activities and $100,000 as financing activities

questions.explanation: ASU 2016-15 (ASC 230-10-45-21C through 21D) clarifies that contingent consideration payments made after a business combination should be bifurcated: the portion up to the acquisition-date fair value ($400,000) is classified as financing activities, and any excess ($100,000) is classified as operating activities. This reflects the view that the original amount is akin to deferred purchase price (financing), while the excess represents a period cost.
questions.explanationJa: ASU 2016-15により、条件付対価の支払いは二分される：取得日公正価値までの金額（$400,000）は財務活動、超過分（$100,000）は営業活動に分類する。原額は繰延購入代金（財務）、超過分は期間費用と見なされる。

questions.stem: Under the indirect method, how does a $15,000 decrease in prepaid expenses affect cash from operating activities?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Added to net income (increases operating cash flow)

choices.label: B
choices.text: Subtracted from net income (decreases operating cash flow)

choices.label: C
choices.text: No effect on operating cash flow

choices.label: D
choices.text: Reported as an investing activity

questions.explanation: A decrease in prepaid expenses (a current asset) means the company used more of its prepaid balance than it replenished—expense was recognized without a corresponding cash payment in the current period. Under the indirect method, decreases in current assets are added to net income because operating cash flow exceeded the expense recognized. The $15,000 is added to net income.
questions.explanationJa: 前払費用（流動資産）の減少は、当期に前払残高の消費が補充を上回ったことを意味する。間接法では流動資産の減少は純利益に加算する。$15,000は営業キャッシュ・フローの増加として加算される。

questions.stem: Drake Corp. pays $200,000 in cash dividends to its common shareholders. How are these dividends classified on the statement of cash flows under U.S. GAAP?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Financing activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: Operating activities

choices.label: C
choices.text: Either operating or financing, at the entity's election

choices.label: D
choices.text: Not reported on the cash flow statement

questions.explanation: Under U.S. GAAP (ASC 230-10-45-15), cash dividends paid to shareholders are classified as financing activities. This is because dividends represent a distribution to the owners of the company—a return of capital or earnings to equity holders. Under IFRS, there is a choice between operating and financing, but U.S. GAAP mandates financing classification.
questions.explanationJa: US GAAPでは、株主への現金配当の支払いは財務活動に分類される（選択不可）。配当は株主（資本提供者）への分配であり、財務活動に該当する。IFRSでは営業または財務の選択が認められるがUS GAAPでは強制。

questions.stem: A U.S. parent has a foreign subsidiary whose functional currency is the euro. When preparing consolidated cash flows, how is the effect of exchange rate changes on the subsidiary's cash balances reported?

choices.label: A
choices.text: As a separate reconciling item between beginning and ending cash balances, distinct from operating, investing, and financing activities

choices.label: B
choices.text: As an operating activity adjustment

choices.label: C
choices.text: Included within each activity section proportionally

choices.label: D
choices.text: Not reported; only the translated amounts are shown

questions.explanation: Under ASC 830-230-45-1, the effect of exchange rate changes on cash held in foreign currencies is reported as a separate item in reconciling the change in cash, cash equivalents, and restricted cash during the period. This line item is presented separately from operating, investing, and financing activities. It explains the difference between the sum of the three activity sections and the actual change in reported cash balances.
questions.explanationJa: 為替レート変動が外貨建て現金残高に与える影響は、営業・投資・財務活動とは別の独立した調整項目として報告する。3つの活動区分の合計と実際の現金残高変動額の差異を説明する項目である。
